title: OpenClaw v2026.5.26: Faster Startup, Transcripts & Voice Control
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.26 eliminates gateway scan overhead, adds transcript infrastructure for meeting-aware SDR pipelines, hardens multi-channel delivery, and ships Realtime Talk voice control.
author: PulseAgent
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.26: Faster Startup, Transcript Pipelines & Realtime Voice Control — What B2B Teams Need to Know

OpenClaw shipped v2026.5.26 on May 27, 2026 — a release focused on three themes that matter directly to production B2B SDR deployments: **speed**, **meeting intelligence**, and **multi-channel reliability**. Here is everything relevant to teams running AI-driven outreach across WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, and Discord.

---

## What Changed in v2026.5.26

### 1. Faster Gateway Startup — No More Redundant Scans

The most immediately noticeable change is gateway startup speed. Previous versions scanned plugins, channels, sessions, usage-cost records, warnings, scheduled services, and filesystem paths on **every boot**. v2026.5.26 eliminates these redundant passes.

For high-load SDR deployments with dozens of active channels, this means:
- Gateway comes online faster after restarts or container re-deploys
- First-message cold-start lag — which caused delayed initial replies to leads — is eliminated
- Plugin metadata is now cached, so repeated operations no longer trigger full re-scans

In production deployments processing hundreds of daily conversations, faster startup translates directly to fewer missed first-contact windows.

### 2. Transcript Infrastructure — Foundation for Meeting-Aware SDR

v2026.5.26 promotes transcript functionality to a **core feature**. Transcripts now back:
- Meeting summaries with source-provider integration
- User turn tracking across long conversations
- Media provenance (images, voice notes, attachments)
- CLI and TUI replay for debugging and auditing

For B2B sales teams, this is the infrastructure layer that enables meeting-to-CRM pipelines: capture a sales call → generate transcript → auto-populate deal notes → trigger follow-up sequences. The plumbing is now in place.

> **Quotable fact**: Transcript-backed meeting summaries reduce post-call CRM entry time by 15–20 minutes per rep, per call — according to teams already using the Meeting Notes plugin from v2026.5.22.

### 3. Multi-Channel Stability — Signal, iMessage, WhatsApp, Discord

All four major messaging channels received production-readiness improvements:

| Channel | What Changed |
|---|---|
| **WhatsApp** | Reaction approval flows; media handling edge cases fixed |
| **Signal** | Reaction approvals; delivery reliability improved |
| **iMessage** | Reaction approvals; media attachment handling |
| **Discord** | Reaction approvals; voice session integration |

Reaction approval flows are especially valuable for SDR workflows: they let the AI propose a reaction (e.g., a thumbs-up acknowledgment) and wait for human approval before sending — adding a human-in-the-loop layer without slowing the entire pipeline.

### 4. Realtime Talk — Voice Call Control

The voice interface now supports:
- **Inspection**: See what the AI is about to say before it speaks
- **Steering**: Redirect the response mid-generation
- **Cancellation**: Kill a response before delivery

Available from both the Web UI and Discord. Wake-name tolerance is also improved — the agent responds to more natural variations of its configured wake phrase.

For teams running voice-based outbound calls or WhatsApp voice notes, this means a human can review and redirect AI voice responses in real time rather than reviewing recordings after the fact.

### 5. Named Authentication Profiles

Credential management gets a significant upgrade: **named authentication profiles** with migration support. SDR teams managing multiple WhatsApp numbers, Telegram bots, or Signal identities across different client accounts can now label and switch between credential sets cleanly.

This eliminates the credential-confusion problem that caused account mixups in multi-tenant deployments.

### 6. Security Hardening — Enterprise-Ready

Three important security fixes landed:

- **SSRF protection**: Browser snapshots (used for lead research) now respect the configured SSRF policy — prevents data exfiltration via crafted URLs
- **Prompt injection defense**: System-event text can no longer spoof prompt markers — closes a class of prompt injection attacks in multi-agent pipelines
- **External content wrapping**: Fetched files are wrapped as external content with proper trust boundaries — stops untrusted web content from influencing agent behavior
- **Device token validation**: Tokens are now validated on connection — hardens multi-device deployments

For B2B teams handling customer data across jurisdictions, these hardening measures are table stakes for enterprise deployments.

### 7. Observability — OpenTelemetry LLM Spans

Diagnostic spans for LLM content are now emitted via OpenTelemetry. Teams with existing observability stacks (Datadog, Grafana, Honeycomb) can now trace AI response latency, token usage, and tool call chains end-to-end.

This makes debugging slow or failed AI responses in production dramatically faster.

### 8. Installation Improvements

- **Alpine Linux** support: Smaller, security-focused Docker images now work out of the box
- **Trusted runtime roots**: Certificate chain handling improved for enterprise network proxies
- **Rastermill replaces Sharp**: Better image processing for media attachments and lead profile photos
- **Codex CLI v0.134.0 bundled**: Embedded AI coding tool available in all installations

---

## Impact Assessment for B2B SDR Teams

| Area | Before v2026.5.26 | After v2026.5.26 |
|---|---|---|
| Gateway startup | Repeated full scans on boot | Cached metadata, fast startup |
| Meeting intelligence | Plugin-level (v2026.5.22) | Core transcript infrastructure |
| Multi-channel stability | Occasional media/reaction issues | Production-hardened |
| Voice control | Send and hope | Inspect, steer, cancel in real time |
| Credential management | Manual per-account config | Named profiles with migration |
| Security | Basic | SSRF + prompt injection hardened |
| Observability | Logs only | Full OTel LLM spans |

## FAQ

**Q: Does the faster startup require any config changes?**
No. Plugin metadata caching and scan elimination happen automatically. No changes to existing config files.

**Q: Is the transcript feature available for WhatsApp voice notes?**
Yes, with the Meeting Notes plugin (introduced in v2026.5.22). The v2026.5.26 infrastructure update makes the transcript layer more stable and adds media provenance — so voice note content is tracked alongside text turns.

**Q: Do I need to migrate existing credentials to use named profiles?**
Existing credentials continue to work as-is. Named profiles are opt-in — you migrate when you choose to. The migration tooling is included in v2026.5.26.

**Q: Which OpenTelemetry backends are supported?**
Any OTLP-compatible backend: Datadog, Honeycomb, Grafana Tempo, Jaeger, Zipkin. Configure the OTLP endpoint in your OpenClaw settings.

**Q: Does Alpine support affect existing Docker deployments?**
No. Existing Debian/Ubuntu-based images are unchanged. Alpine is an additional supported base — useful for teams optimizing container image size.
